product_overview: Automated alumni tracking system that manages alumni master data, runs source-prioritized public searches, disambiguates candidates, cross-validates findings, and stores auditable evidence. The implementation exposes CRUD for alumni and endpoints to trigger tracking for one or all alumni (manual or scheduled).
core_goals: Maintain a master list of alumni with opt-out support and basic CRUD operations.
Generate contextual search profiles per alumni (name variations, affiliation and context keywords) as defined in the pseudocode (pages 2-4).
Run prioritized, scheduled or manual tracking jobs that gather candidate signals from public sources, disambiguate results, and compute confidence scores (pages 3-7).
Persist tracking results and evidence as auditable traces and notify admins on significant status changes (pages 7-9).
Provide explicit API operations to trigger tracking for a single alumni or all eligible alumni for integration with a scheduler (codebase endpoints).

features.name: Alumni Profiling (CRUD + Opt-Out)
features.description: Create, list and manage alumni master records, including toggling opt-out. This implements the master data described in the design (see 'LANGKAH 1 — Siapkan Profil Target' pseudocode on pages 2-3). Opt-out prevents tracking jobs from running for that alumni.
user_flows: POST /api/alumni/ with valid alumni payload (name, year_in, year_out, program, etc.) -> Receive 201 Created with created alumni object including id -> GET /api/alumni/ -> Receive 200 with list containing the created alumni (happy path)
POST /api/alumni/ with malformed payload (missing required fields) -> Receive 400 with validation error (error flow)

PUT /api/alumni/{alumni_id}/opt_out with opt_out=true -> Receive 200 with updated alumni record showing opt_out=true -> POST /api/trigger_tracking/{alumni_id} -> Receive 400 with message indicating 'opt-out' prevents tracking (error flow demonstrating opt-out behavior)
PUT /api/alumni/{alumni_id}/opt_out for non-existent alumni_id -> Receive 404 Not Found (error flow)

features.name: Manual Tracking Trigger (single alumni)
features.description: Allow an admin or operator to request the system run the full tracking pipeline for a specific alumni (profile assembly, query generation, source retrieval, signal extraction, disambiguation, cross-validation, and evidence persistence). This maps to the endpoint POST /api/trigger_tracking/{alumni_id} and the tracking logic in services/tracking_logic.py (see scheduler and job flow on pages 3-7).
user_flows: POST /api/trigger_tracking/{alumni_id} with valid alumni_id -> Receive 200 with job acceptance and a tracking result summary (status, top candidates, skor_confidence, evidence_id) -> GET /api/alumni/ -> Receive 200 with updated alumni status field (e.g., 'Teridentifikasi dari Sumber Publik' or 'Perlu Verifikasi Manual') (happy path)
POST /api/trigger_tracking/{alumni_id} for alumni with opt_out=true -> Receive 400 with an error indicating tracking is not permitted for opted-out alumni (error flow)
POST /api/trigger_tracking/{alumni_id} with invalid alumni_id format -> Receive 400 or 422 with validation error (error flow)

POST /api/trigger_tracking/{alumni_id} when external sources are unreachable/rate-limited -> Receive 503 or 500 with error detail and retry suggestion; tracking job should be idempotent/reschedulable (error flow covering external failures)

features.name: Bulk / Scheduler Tracking Trigger (all eligible alumni)
features.description: Endpoint to trigger tracking for all eligible alumni (used by the weekly scheduler described in 'LANGKAH 3 — Jalankan Job Pelacakan Berkala' on page 3). The endpoint iterates eligible alumni (status = 'Belum Dilacak' OR 'Perlu Verifikasi Manual' OR last updated > 180 days) and runs the same tracking pipeline per alumni.
user_flows: POST /api/trigger_tracking_all with no body -> Receive 200 with summary { processed_count, skipped_opt_out_count, errors: [] } -> Subsequent GET /api/alumni/ -> Receive 200 showing updated statuses for processed alumni (happy path for scheduled run)
POST /api/trigger_tracking_all when there are zero eligible alumni -> Receive 200 with processed_count=0 and message 'no eligible alumni' (informational error flow)

POST /api/trigger_tracking_all during a partial outage of an external source -> Receive 200 with processed_count and errors array listing failed alumni ids; failed items are flagged for retry in next scheduled job (error flow)

features.name: Tracking Logic: Profile generation, Query building, Source retrieval, Disambiguation, Cross-validation and Evidence Storage
features.description: Implements the core pipeline steps described across pages 2-7: assemble profile (name variants, affiliation/context keywords), determine source priorities, generate search queries (see 'LANGKAH 4 — Hasilkan Query Pencarian' on page 4), retrieve results from prioritized sources (page 4-5), extract signals, compute confidence via disambiguation (page 5-6), cross-validate across sources (page 7), and persist an auditable 'jejak bukti' (page 7). This logic is invoked by the trigger endpoints in the codebase and implemented in services/tracking_logic.py.
user_flows: POST /api/trigger_tracking/{alumni_id} -> Receive 200 with detailed tracking_result containing { status, ringkasan_info, kandidat_kuat (if any), perlu_verifikasi (if any), evidence_id } where evidence_id references saved jejak bukti in DB (happy path showing end-to-end pipeline and evidence persistence)

POST /api/trigger_tracking/{alumni_id} where generated query_list yields no candidates -> Receive 200 with status 'Belum Ditemukan di Sumber Publik' and evidence_id logged with empty candidates (happy path - no matches)

POST /api/trigger_tracking/{alumni_id} and during cross-validation a conflict is detected (contradictory affiliations) -> Receive 200 with status 'Perlu Verifikasi Manual', top_kandidat list and flag_review=true recorded in evidence (error/verification-needed flow described in 'LANGKAH 9' on page 7)

POST /api/trigger_tracking/{alumni_id} and a downstream database write fails -> Receive 500 with error; tracking job should return a retryable error and not leave partial/untracked state (error flow covering persistence failures)

features.name: Admin Review & Notification
features.description: After tracking runs, results that require human verification are surfaced for admin review (see 'Perlu Verifikasi Manual' flows on pages 6-9). The system stores change history and triggers notifications to admins when status changes significantly (page 7-8). Admin review is expected to be performed via an admin UI reading the stored evidence and candidate lists (API endpoints for UI are the existing alumni list and tracking-result payloads returned by trigger endpoints).
user_flows: POST /api/trigger_tracking/{alumni_id} -> Receive 200 with tracking_result.status = 'Perlu Verifikasi Manual' and top_kandidat list -> Admin opens admin UI and requests details (UI calls GET /api/alumni/ and internal endpoint to fetch evidence by evidence_id) -> Receive 200 with evidence and candidate details for manual review (happy path for review flow)

POST /api/trigger_tracking/{alumni_id} -> Receive 200 with status change from previous status -> System sends notification to admin (internal notification action) -> Admin does not take action within threshold -> Scheduler re-enqueues the alumni for the next run (error / monitoring flow)

endpoints.description: Returns a list of all alumni
Update opt_out status for an alumni
Trigger test tracking logic for a specific alumni id
Trigger test tracking logic for all valid alumni records
